channel.title: ClosurePressure on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in ClosurePressure on emsenn.net

item.title: Closure Condition
item.description:
A closure condition is a description of a state where specific gaps in the predicate graph are resolved. Goals are named closure conditions.

Closure conditions are not imposed by external stakeholders. They arise from the system’s own unsatisfied closures: when the predicate graph reports axiom violations, those violations generate closure pressure, and the pressure produces closure conditions.

A closure condition is met when its constituent axioms are satisfied with evidence. The satisfaction deficit measures how far the system is from meeting all closure conditions.

item.title: Closure Condition
item.description:
A closure condition is a description of a state where specific gaps in the predicate graph are resolved. Goals are named closure conditions.

Closure conditions are not imposed by external stakeholders. They arise from the system’s own unsatisfied closures: when the predicate graph reports axiom violations, those violations generate closure pressure, and the pressure produces closure conditions.

A closure condition is met when its constituent axioms are satisfied with evidence. The satisfaction deficit measures how far the system is from meeting all closure conditions.

item.title: Milestone
item.description:
A milestone is a named state where a set of closure conditions are simultaneously met. It represents a meaningful intermediate closure — a state where enough gaps have been resolved that a new capability exists.

Milestones are declared as milestone: values in plan frontmatter. Plans that share a milestone value form a coherent group whose collective completion constitutes the named state.

Milestones are not deadlines. They are descriptions of closure states, ordered by dependency, not by calendar.

item.title: Milestone
item.description:
A milestone is a named state where a set of closure conditions are simultaneously met. It represents a meaningful intermediate closure — a state where enough gaps have been resolved that a new capability exists.

Milestones are declared as milestone: values in plan frontmatter. Plans that share a milestone value form a coherent group whose collective completion constitutes the named state.

Milestones are not deadlines. They are descriptions of closure states, ordered by dependency, not by calendar.
